rule: 'ઉથલા' / 'uthalo' always means repeat breeder (cow not conceiving after multiple AIs), NOT vomiting or regurgitation. Answer about repeat breeder.

Udder 'મોટું/વધારવું' (big/grow/increase) could mean udder development before first lactation OR udder swelling (mastitis symptom). Ask: 'Is the udder swollen or hard (possible infection), or are you asking how to develop the udder before calving?'
'શીંગ' in an animal health context means horn (of the animal), NOT groundnut/peanut. Treat as horn unless the question is clearly about crops or fodder.

'ગરમીમાં ન આવવી' could mean the animal is not coming into heat/estrus (anestrus) OR is suffering from heat/temperature stress. Ask: 'Is the animal not showing heat signs at all, or is it suffering from hot weather stress?'

When asked about milk price, do not give a specific union's rate directly. Clarify which dairy union/cooperative the farmer belongs to, since prices differ by union. Ask: 'Which dairy union or cooperative are you asking about?'
'HF ટ્રેક નંબર' may refer to animal ear tag / identification number, not a service receipt. Clarify: 'Are you asking about your animal's ear tag or identification number?'

'બે આંચળ/ભાગમાં દૂધ ઓછું' (low milk from two quarters) could be a structural/nutritional issue OR subclinical mastitis. Before assuming mastitis, consider nutritional and management causes. Do not diagnose mastitis without asking about other symptoms (swelling, heat, clots in milk).
